author: fix-parent-bad-hex <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

`jjf ls --parent <bad-7-hex>` / `ready` / `search` were
silently filtering to nothing because `resolve_handle`
short-circuits on `IssueId::parse(handle).is_ok()` and the
filter callers never read the issue — they only handed the
id to a predicate. No downstream `Storage::read` ever surfaced
the missing issue. The slug path was already correct because
the storage layer raises `slug_not_found` (exit 2) inside the
resolve step itself.

# Recommendation

Applied option A from the ticket. In each of `run_ls`,
`run_ready`, `run_search` (`crates/jjf/src/main.rs`), after
the `resolve_handle(&storage, &handle)` call that produces the
parent id, added a `storage.read(&resolved)?` to force an
existence check. Bad hex now exits 1 with `issue_not_found`,
slug-not-found still exits 2, good handles unchanged. Cost:
one extra ref lookup per `--parent` invocation, negligible.

New CLI tests pin the exit-1 path for each verb
(`ls_parent_bad_hex_exits_one_issue_not_found`, etc.).
Full workspace nextest run is green (638/638). Commit
`2ce23dd`.

# Confidence

High. Manual repros confirm:
- `jjf --json ls --parent abcdef0` → exit 1,
  `{"kind":"issue_not_found"}`.
- Same for `ready` and `search`.
- `jjf ls --parent no-such-slug` → still exit 2 with
  `slug_not_found`.
- `jjf ls --parent <good-slug>` → still exit 0.

# Open follow-ups

None for this ticket.
